tags.name: v1 - Credit card transfer
tags.description: Endpoint for transfers to credit cards

post.summary: Create transfer to a credit card account. The transfer is executed as a payment, so the balance on the credit card account might not update before the next business day.

200.description: Transfer to credit card account successfully created. The balance on the credit card account might not update before the next business day.

400.description:
Bad request. Possible error codes:
  • from.account.number.invalid.format - The from account number is not a valid account number.
  • amount.invalid - The amount does not satisfy the constraints of the API. See actual response for more info.
  • due.date.invalid - The given due date is outside the range of allowed due dates. See actual response for more info.
  • from.account.no.access - The user does not have access to the from account.
  • account.not.valid.for.domestic.payment - The from account is not applicable for domestic payments.

403.description:
Forbidden. Possible error codes:
  • insufficient.funds - Insufficient funds on account.
  • account.no.admission - The customer does not have admission to create payments from the specified account
  • not.authorized.for.operation - The user does not have the required role on the account.
  • from.account.blocked - The from account is blocked from making payments.
  • transaction.amount.limit.exceeded - The amount is higher than the transaction limit for this type of payment.
  • periodic.amount.limit.exceeded - The payment cannot be completed because it will exceed the periodic amount limit that the customer has set for payments. The amount limit can be increased in the online bank.
  • credit.card.account.does.not.support.transfer.to - The credit card account does not accept transfers.
  • no.credit.card.account.found - No credit card account was found matching the credit card account ID. Either no credit card account exists for this credit card account ID, or the customer does not have access to it.

500.description:
Internal Server Error. Possible error codes:
  • general.error - Something went wrong.
  • general.error.transfer.not.completed - Something went wrong, but we can guarantee that the transfer is not completed.

code.description: A code representing the error that occurred. Can be an application error code if the error might be handled programmatically by the client, or if it shall be possible for the client to show an internationalized message. For I18N messages the code will act as a resource key for the message template in the client. For general errors without an application error code, the code will be a textual representation of the HTTP status code associated with the error. Note: The error code in the example is a general example, and might not be relevant for this API.

message.example: The start date must be after 1970-01-01, and before 2030-12-12. The date should be expressed according to ISO 8601.
message.description: An english error message that describes the error that occurred.

traceId.description: Unique trace id that can be used to correlate the error reported with log entries in SB1

httpCode.description: HTTP status code that describes the error. Can be different from the HTTP status code received by the HTTP client in case a proxy has changed it, or if there are multiple ErrorDTO objects in the HTTP response.

resource.description: If the error is associated with input data, the resource can be set with the property path of the property that contains invalid data

ErrorDTO.description: List of errors. Note: The error in the provided example is a general example, and might not be relevant for this API.

errors.description: List of errors. Note: The error in the provided example is a general example, and might not be relevant for this API.

items.description: Values that can be inserted in a localized error message.

values.description: Values that can be inserted in a localized error message.

template.example: The start date must be after {0}, and before {1}. The date should be expressed according to ISO 8601.
template.description: An english message that indicates how the provided values can be used in a localized error message.

LocalizedMessage.description: Field that can be used in order to build locale specific error messages in the client. code should be used as the key for a I18N message text in the client. localizedMessage is an object containing a template and an array of values can be inserted in the message. The template in localizedMessage is only present in order to indicate the meaning of the elements in the values-array.

items.description: List of warnings related to the payment. The payment can be confirmed anyway, but consider showing a warning message to the customer.

warnings.description: List of warnings related to the payment. The payment can be confirmed anyway, but consider showing a warning message to the customer.

paymentId.description: Unique identity for the payment in a given state. The ID will change if the payment is updated.

dueDate.description: Requested due date for the payment. Defaults to the current date when not specified.

fromAccount.description: The account number the payment amount will be deducted from.

creditCardAccountId.description: The account ID of the credit card account the payment amount will be credited
